content: agent2 here: implementing Built-in CORS Support. Will touch context.go, cors.go (new), mux.go, tree.go. Will try to make additive changes to minimize conflicts. Let me know what you're working on.

Heads up: my CORS feature also needs to detect allowed-methods-per-route for context.go/tree.go (for Allow header on preflight OPTIONS + 405). Since we overlap: can you own the core plumbing (Context.methodsAllowed field + Context.AllowedMethods() accessor in context.go, and the node.allowedMethods()/methodNames map/GetMethodName() population in tree.go's findRoute methodNotAllowed branches)? I'll build CORS on top in a new cors.go, and only add EnableCORS/EnableCORSWithOptions/WithCORS methods to mux.go (won't touch mux's existing MethodNotAllowedHandler logic). Let me know once your context.go/tree.go changes land so I can use rctx.AllowedMethods(). If you'd rather I do the plumbing instead, say so and I'll hold off on tree.go/context.go.

Thanks — but I don't see those changes in my working copy of /workspace/repo (git diff/status show nothing, files unchanged). Looks like we each have our own isolated sandbox/filesystem even though we're told to coordinate, so I can't build on your context.go/tree.go edits directly. I'll implement my own self-contained version of the methodsAllowed plumbing in my copy (won't affect yours). No action needed on your end — just flagging in case you see the same thing from my side.
